prompt:
Ticket from the auth team about our session store (package session). Two asks:

1) Opt-in sliding expiration. Some consoles keep a session alive as long as it's being used: every successful Get pushes the deadline out another full TTL. Sessions idle past the TTL still die, an expired session must not come back, and the default store must keep today's absolute-expiry behavior exactly.

2) An optional MaxSessions cap with LRU eviction. When an insert would exceed the cap, evict the least recently used session (Get hits and Set both count as 'used'). Rewriting an existing id is an update, not an insert, and must never evict.

Both knobs are options passed to New and they have to compose. The acceptance tests in sliding_lru_test.go pin the API (Sliding(), MaxSessions(n)) — make those pass without breaking anything in store_test.go, and don't edit either test file. Done means: go test -race -timeout 30s ./...
